windows.transcript: architecture. So we probably should have scented the whole thing because as is, you won't be able to smell anything until you've already used up over
windows.embed_text_v2: Melt wax in saucepan on hot-plate, pour into blue silicone cube mould to cast ice-cube shapes, then layer green wax and the wax cubes into a salt-rimmed margarita glass around a central wick.
windows.summary_v2: A novelty candle is made to look like a margarita: wax melted on a hot-plate is poured into a silicone cube mould for ice-cube pieces, then layered with green wax inside a salt-rimmed margarita glass.

tags_v2.action: pour wax into glass and silicone mould

seconds_v2.desc: Reveal shot: finished green margarita-style candle in a salt-rimmed margarita glass with a white wick (straw) on a kitchen counter.
Overhead view of a small black hot-plate burner with a steel saucepan holding a melting chunk of off-white wax.
Same hot-plate from above: the wax has fully melted into a clear/dark liquid in the saucepan.
Top-down of a blue silicone 6-cavity cube mould on the wood counter; the black saucepan of melted wax enters from the left.
Pouring melted wax from the saucepan into the blue silicone cube mould to cast clear/translucent ice-cube-shaped pieces.
Macro shot of the salt-rimmed margarita glass: a single cast wax ice-cube sits in green wax, with white wax being poured down a central wick.
Continued pour: more white wax is being added through the center of the green-filled glass holding the first ice-cube piece.
Multiple wax ice cubes have been arranged on top of the green wax in the margarita glass while pouring continues to lock them in.
Hand steadies the finished margarita candle by its stem: green wax body topped with wax ice cubes and a salt-rim, wick rising from center.
